name: clawdcontext
displayName: ClawdContext — Markdown OS for AI Agents
description: The complete VS Code toolkit for agentic AI: context health (CER dashboard), Markdown OS diagnostics, lessons governance, SKILL.md security scanning, Skill Forge Studio (guided skill creation across 6 domains × 7 archetypes), multi-provider AI support (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Ollama, DeepSeek), and enterprise-ready safety — all offline-first with zero telemetry.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Read-only analysis works in untrusted workspaces. File-generating and refactor commands should be used in trusted workspaces.

commands.title: ClawdContext: Compare CER with Previous Commit
ClawdContext: Create Skill with Skill Forge

viewsWelcome.contents:
No Markdown OS files detected.
[Scaffold Templates](command:clawdcontext.scaffoldMarkdownOS)
[Analyze Workspace](command:clawdcontext.analyzeWorkspace)
[Create Skill with Skill Forge](command:clawdcontext.skillForge)

clawdcontext.tokenBudget.description: Context window size override in tokens. Leave at default to auto-detect from your configured AI model.

clawdcontext.cerWarningThreshold.description: CER threshold below which a warning is shown (0.0 - 1.0)

clawdcontext.cerCriticalThreshold.description: CER threshold below which a critical alert is shown (0.0 - 1.0)

clawdcontext.lessonsTtlDays.description: TTL in days for local-heuristic lessons before staleness warning

clawdcontext.lessonsMaxEntries.description: Maximum recommended lessons before bloat warning

clawdcontext.alwaysLoadedFiles.description: Files always loaded into context (counted toward CER)

clawdcontext.onDemandPatterns.description: Glob patterns for on-demand files (not counted as always-loaded)

clawdcontext.enableCodeLens.description: Show inline CodeLens on lessons.md entries

clawdcontext.enableStatusBar.description: Show CER and context health in the status bar

clawdcontext.securityAllowlist.description: Security rule codes to suppress (e.g. ["SEC_EXEC_SHELL", "SEC_CRED_ENV"]). Use for first-party skills with legitimate shell commands or env vars.
clawdcontext.securityAllowlist.markdownDescription:
Security rule codes to suppress entirely. Available codes:
- `SEC_EXFIL_FETCH` — HTTP fetch to external URLs
- `SEC_EXFIL_DNS` — DNS exfiltration
- `SEC_INJECT_HIDDEN` — Encoded injection
- `SEC_INJECT_ROLE` — Role override
- `SEC_INJECT_DELIMITER` — Delimiter manipulation
- `SEC_CRED_ENV` — Env var access
- `SEC_CRED_FILE` — Sensitive file access
- `SEC_EXEC_SHELL` — Shell commands
- `SEC_EXEC_EVAL` — Dynamic eval
- `SEC_PERSIST` — Persistence
- `SEC_STEALTH_COMMENTS` — Hidden comments
- `SEC_OBFUSC` — Code obfuscation
- `SEC_RECON` — System reconnaissance

clawdcontext.trustedDomains.description: Domains to trust in URL pattern matching (e.g. ["clawdcontext.com", "github.com"]). URLs matching these domains won't trigger SEC_EXFIL_FETCH.
clawdcontext.trustedDomains.markdownDescription:
Domains to trust for URL-based security patterns. URLs containing these domains are excluded from `SEC_EXFIL_FETCH` findings.

Example: `["mycompany.com", "github.com"]`

clawdcontext.securityCodeBlockAware.description: Suppress security findings inside markdown fenced code blocks (``` ... ```). These are documentation examples, not executable threats.

enumDescriptions: Ollama (local models — no API key needed)
clawdcontext.ai.provider.description: AI provider for optional intelligent features. Set to 'none' to keep the extension fully offline.
clawdcontext.ai.provider.markdownDescription:
AI provider for optional intelligent features.

- `none` — AI disabled (default, fully offline)
- `openai` — [OpenAI API](https://platform.openai.com/)
- `anthropic` — [Anthropic API](https://console.anthropic.com/)
- `azure-openai` — [Azure OpenAI](https://azure.microsoft.com/products/ai-services/openai-service) (enterprise)
- `ollama` — [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) (local, no API key needed)
- `deepseek` — [DeepSeek](https://platform.deepseek.com/) (affordable, high quality)

clawdcontext.ai.apiKey.description: API key for OpenAI or Anthropic. Not needed for Ollama. Stored in VS Code settings (use Secrets API or env var for production).
clawdcontext.ai.apiKey.markdownDescription:
API key for your AI provider.

- **OpenAI**: starts with `sk-`
- **Anthropic**: starts with `sk-ant-`
- **Ollama**: leave empty (no key needed)

clawdcontext.ai.model.description: Model name override. Leave empty for defaults: gpt-4.1-mini (OpenAI/Azure), claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (Anthropic), llama3.2 (Ollama), deepseek-chat (DeepSeek).
clawdcontext.ai.model.markdownDescription:
Model name override. Leave empty for smart defaults:

| Provider | Default Model |
|---|---|
| OpenAI | `gpt-4.1-mini` |
| Anthropic | `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` |
| Azure OpenAI | `gpt-4.1-mini` |
| Ollama | `llama3.2` |
| DeepSeek | `deepseek-chat` |

clawdcontext.ai.baseUrl.description: Custom base URL for the AI API. Useful for enterprise proxies, Azure OpenAI, or self-hosted endpoints. Leave empty for defaults.
clawdcontext.ai.baseUrl.markdownDescription:
Custom base URL override.

Defaults:
- OpenAI: `https://api.openai.com`
- Anthropic: `https://api.anthropic.com`
- Ollama: `http://localhost:11434`

Examples:
- Azure OpenAI: `https://my-resource.openai.azure.com`
- Enterprise proxy: `https://ai-proxy.mycompany.com`

clawdcontext.ai.caCertPath.description: Path to a PEM certificate bundle for enterprise TLS/SSL. Required when behind corporate proxies with custom CA certificates (e.g., ZScaler, Netskope).
clawdcontext.ai.caCertPath.markdownDescription:
Path to a PEM CA certificate bundle file.

Required for enterprise environments with custom CA certificates (e.g., ZScaler, Netskope, Bluecoat).

The certificate is merged with system root CAs so both enterprise and public endpoints work.

Example: `/etc/ssl/certs/enterprise-ca.pem` or `~/certs/corp-ca-bundle.pem`

clawdcontext.ai.rejectUnauthorized.description: Verify TLS certificates. Set to false only for debugging with self-signed certificates. NOT recommended for production.
clawdcontext.ai.rejectUnauthorized.markdownDescription:
Verify TLS/SSL certificates.

⚠️ Set to `false` **only** for debugging with self-signed certs. This disables certificate validation entirely.

For enterprise proxies, prefer `clawdcontext.ai.caCertPath` instead.

clawdcontext.ai.timeout.description: AI request timeout in milliseconds. Increase for large prompts or slow connections.
clawdcontext.ai.timeout.markdownDescription:
AI request timeout in milliseconds.

- Default: `30000` (30 seconds)
- Ollama with large models: consider `60000`+
- Enterprise proxies: consider `45000`+

clawdcontext.ai.maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens in AI responses. Higher values give more detailed responses but cost more.
clawdcontext.ai.maxTokens.markdownDescription:
Maximum tokens in AI responses.

- Default: `4000`
- For generation tasks: `8000`+
- For quick validations: `2000` is sufficient

clawdcontext.ai.temperature.description: AI response creativity (0 = deterministic, 1 = creative, 2 = very creative). Recommended: 0.3 for analysis, 0.7 for generation.
clawdcontext.ai.temperature.markdownDescription:
AI response temperature.

- `0.0` — Deterministic, consistent
- `0.3` — Low creativity (recommended for validation/analysis)
- `0.7` — Moderate creativity (good for generation)
- `1.0`+ — High creativity (experimental)

clawdcontext.ai.azureDeployment.description: Azure OpenAI deployment name. Required when provider is 'azure-openai'.
clawdcontext.ai.azureDeployment.markdownDescription:
Azure OpenAI deployment name.

Required when `clawdcontext.ai.provider` is `azure-openai`.

This is the deployment name you created in Azure AI Studio, not the model name.

Example: `my-gpt4o-deployment`

clawdcontext.ai.azureApiVersion.description: Azure OpenAI API version. Only used with 'azure-openai' provider.

clawdcontext.ai.certPath.description: Path to PEM client certificate for mTLS authentication. Used with enterprise proxies requiring mutual TLS.
clawdcontext.ai.certPath.markdownDescription:
Path to PEM client certificate for mTLS.

Use with `clawdcontext.ai.keyPath` for mutual TLS authentication.

For PFX/P12 format, use `clawdcontext.ai.pfxPath` instead.

clawdcontext.ai.keyPath.description: Path to PEM private key for mTLS authentication. Paired with certPath.
clawdcontext.ai.keyPath.markdownDescription:
Path to PEM private key for mTLS.

Must be paired with `clawdcontext.ai.certPath`.

clawdcontext.ai.pfxPath.description: Path to PFX/P12 certificate bundle for mTLS. Alternative to separate cert+key PEM files.
clawdcontext.ai.pfxPath.markdownDescription:
Path to PFX/P12 certificate bundle for mTLS.

Alternative to `certPath` + `keyPath` when you have a combined `.pfx` or `.p12` file.

If password-protected, set `clawdcontext.ai.pfxPassphrase`.

clawdcontext.ai.pfxPassphrase.description: Passphrase for the PFX/P12 certificate. Only needed if your PFX file is password-protected.

clawdcontext.skillForge.serverUrl.markdownDescription:
URL of the Skill Forge Studio backend.

Default: `http://localhost:8742`

Change this if you run the backend on a different host or port.

clawdcontext.skillForge.apiKey.description: Optional API key for the Skill Forge Studio backend (if protected).

clawdcontext.skillForge.generateTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for AI-powered skill generation (default: 180s).
clawdcontext.skillForge.generateTimeout.markdownDescription:
Timeout in seconds for AI-powered skill generation.

AI generation makes multiple LLM calls and can take 30–120s. Increase if you see timeout errors.

Default: `180` (3 minutes)

clawdcontext.skillForge.autoStart.description: Automatically start the Skill Forge Studio backend when the extension activates.
clawdcontext.skillForge.autoStart.markdownDescription:
Automatically start the Skill Forge Studio Python backend when the extension activates.

Requires `skill_forge_studio/` with a Python venv in the workspace.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with ClawdContext
walkthroughs.description: Set up your AI agent's Markdown OS for optimal context efficiency.

steps.title: Analyze Your Workspace
steps.description:
Scan your project for CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, lessons.md, and other Markdown OS files.

[Analyze Workspace](command:clawdcontext.analyzeWorkspace)

steps.title: Open the CER Dashboard
steps.description:
View your Context Efficiency Ratio, layer distribution, positional attention map, and security findings.

[Open Dashboard](command:clawdcontext.showDashboard)

steps.title: Scaffold Markdown OS Templates
steps.description:
Generate starter templates for CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, todo.md, and lessons.md.

[Scaffold Templates](command:clawdcontext.scaffoldMarkdownOS)

steps.title: Enable AI Features (Optional)
steps.description:
Connect an AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Ollama, DeepSeek) for intelligent validation, generation, and contradiction detection.

[Configure AI](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22clawdcontext.ai.provider%22)

steps.title: Run Security Scan
steps.description:
Lint your SKILL.md files for prompt injection, credential exposure, and supply-chain risks.

[Run Lint](command:clawdcontext.lintMdFiles)

scripts.check: npm run compile && npm run lint && npm test
scripts.package:ci: npm run compile && npm test && vsce package --no-yarn
